summary: A sorting relation where multiple object classes must end in their matching named regions.
constraints: Declare explicit object name lists in the registry, e.g. self.red_particle_names and self.blue_particle_names.
Declare explicit region names, e.g. red_bin and blue_bin, and make bins sensor=True unless they are physical containers.
Do not use abstract targets like any_particle; every subgoal must reference concrete registered names or a deterministic list in check_objective.
For simple two-object sorting, Tier 5 can be required; for many particles or field-force sorting, Tier 4 progress may be honest until a stronger validator exists.
Use deterministic code-level truth: every red object is in red_bin and every blue object is in blue_bin, or measurable progress toward that condition.
repair_guidance: If the validator sees abstract aliases, replace them with concrete object names or explicit registry lists.
If particles drift/fall, add stable support or switch to zero_g/field-force capability deliberately.
If the task has many particles, decompose into representative subgoals plus check_objective over all objects.
